reasoning: Shantanu Thakur is Matua — one of us — and he sits as a minister in Delhi now. That means something. The schemes Mamata gave me are good, yes, but in 2020 when everything broke, the panchayat people stole the relief money anyway. Didi's hand does not reach past the corrupt men at the village level. I am still afraid of NRC, but five years have passed and it has not happened here. Thakur is proof that our community can have real power, not just promises. My fear of NRC is old fear now. My fear of hunger is today's fear.
primary_drivers: Shantanu Thakur represents Matua political power and presence in central government
2020 experience revealed that TMC welfare schemes are undermined by local corruption anyway
NRC fear has not materialized for five years; community representation feels more tangible now than protection from distant laws
